2026-04-17T07:19:35.3028669Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3029548Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3029992Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3030351Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3030670Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3030910Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3031077Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3031268Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3031445Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3031681Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3031891Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3032178Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T07:19:35.3032437Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T07:23:02.8945668Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:20:42.387734954Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58268,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2:\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation.\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n- **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars.\\n- **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400.\\n- **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\u0026#34; It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn\\u0026#39;t mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn\\u0026#39;t mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs.\\n- No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n#### 1a. Add a \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection under Key concepts\\n\\nAfter the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nThe `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is\\ncreated with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and\\nInputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found`\\n(indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible.\\n\\nWhen no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section\\n\\nReplace the existing bullet list with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains\\n  ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any\\n  supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id`\\n  request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters).\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state\\n  (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an\\n  `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `\\u0026#34;Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.\\u0026#34;`.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was\\n  created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not\\n  match the key provided in this request.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has\\n  already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section\\n\\nReplace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage call logging\\n\\nWhen the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`),\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and\\n`Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and latency\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`,\\n  `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry)\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Information\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are\\nexplanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is\\ndesired, add a new test file:\\n\\n**Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs`\\n\\nWrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion`\\nand place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README\\u0026#39;s new \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all\\nrequired sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no\\nsections need to be created from scratch.\\n\\nCurrent structure (all present):\\n1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection added\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24552776867/agentic_workflow) · ● 371.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58268\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T07:17:58Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T07:17:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-17T07:23:02.9006066Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:20:42.58005446Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58268,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2:\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation.\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n- **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars.\\n- **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400.\\n- **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only \\u0026#34;The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\u0026#34; It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn\\u0026#39;t mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn\\u0026#39;t mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs.\\n- No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n#### 1a. Add a \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection under Key concepts\\n\\nAfter the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nThe `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is\\ncreated with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and\\nInputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found`\\n(indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible.\\n\\nWhen no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section\\n\\nReplace the existing bullet list with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains\\n  ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any\\n  supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id`\\n  request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters).\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state\\n  (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an\\n  `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `\\u0026#34;Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.\\u0026#34;`.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was\\n  created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not\\n  match the key provided in this request.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has\\n  already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section\\n\\nReplace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage call logging\\n\\nWhen the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`),\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and\\n`Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and latency\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`,\\n  `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry)\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;LogLevel\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;Azure.AI.AgentServer\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;Information\\u0026#34;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are\\nexplanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is\\ndesired, add a new test file:\\n\\n**Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs`\\n\\nWrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion`\\nand place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README\\u0026#39;s new \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all\\nrequired sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no\\nsections need to be created from scratch.\\n\\nCurrent structure (all present):\\n1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection added\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24552776867/agentic_workflow) · ● 371.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58268\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T07:17:58Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T07:17:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-17T07:23:02.9496472Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-17T07:23:02.9496676Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-17T07:23:02.9496994Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-04-17T07:23:02.9497105Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
